title: 10 best herbal teas for over bleeding during periods

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose teas helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural antispasmodic and astringent properties. The tea's active compounds, such as rutin and quercetin, help to constrict blood vessels and reduce capillary fragility, thereby decreasing menstrual flow. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its ability to alleviate heavy bleeding by reducing inflammation in the uterine lining. As a result, guelder rose teas can provide relief from excessive bleeding and associated discomfort during menstruation.
remedy_properties: Astringent compounds: The tea's astringent compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, help to reduce blood loss by contracting the uterine muscles and constricting the blood vessels, thereby slowing down bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Guelder Rose tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm the uterus, reducing inflammation and spasms that may contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.

Antioxidant activity: The tea's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and flavonoids, help to protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive bleeding, promoting overall uterine health and reducing menstrual cramps and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Guelder Rose contain tannins and flavonoids, which help to contract blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding during menstruation.
Barks: The barks of Guelder Rose contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help to prevent oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, reducing the severity of menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh viburnum opulus leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Rinse the harvested plant material with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Combine one teaspoon of dried viburnum opulus leaves and flowers with 200ml boiling water for five minutes steeping.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup discarding solids carefully.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before drinking in small amounts as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover teas helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains isoflavones, which have been shown to mimic the body's natural estrogen levels. This helps to regulate blood flow and reduce heavy bleeding associated with menstruation. Additionally, red clover has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the uterine lining, further reducing excessive bleeding. As a result, red clover teas may provide relief from distressing symptoms of menorrhagia, making it a natural and effective remedy for women experiencing heavy periods.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover tea contains isoflavones, which have been shown to help reduce blood flow and ease menopausal symptoms, including heavy bleeding during periods. This is because isoflavones work as natural estrogen receptors in the body, regulating hormonal imbalances that can contribute to heavy bleeding.

Vitamin K: Red clover tea is a rich source of vitamin K, which plays a crucial role in blood clotting and platelet function. Adequate vitamin K levels can help reduce menstrual bleeding by promoting healthy coagulation and preventing excessive bleeding.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in red clover tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy bleeding during periods.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and isoflavones, which help to tone and constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive menstrual flow.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, they can help to reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, alleviating heavy bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried red clover flowers of high quality according to your trusted supplier or gather them yourself sustainably.
Measure one teaspoon of dried red clover flowers and add it to a clean glass teapot.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 212°f and pour it evenly over the red clover flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the bioactive compounds and essential oils.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with over bleeding during periods because its soothing properties can help to ease menstrual cramps, reduce inflammation, and relax the uterine muscles. This can lead to a decrease in heavy bleeding and clotting. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may also help to reduce swelling and irritation within the uterus, allowing for a more normal and manageable flow.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Chamomile tea helps to constrict blood vessels, which can reduce excessive bleeding during menstruation by reducing blood flow to the uterus.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to soothe and calm the uterine lining, reducing inflammation that may contribute to heavy or prolonged bleeding.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic effects can help to relax uterine muscles, which can reduce cramping and spasms that may exacerbate bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce heavy menstrual flow and alleviate cramps.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have natural anticoagulant properties, which can help to regulate blood clotting and reduce excessive bleeding during periods.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried chamomile flowers and measure out one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Heat fresh filtered water in a tea kettle or pot until it reaches boiling point then reduce heat to simmer.
Steep the chamomile flowers in the hot water for 5-7 minutes allowing their soothing properties to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes then drink it slowly throughout the day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai teas helps with over bleeding during periods because it has a profound effect on the uterine blood vessels, helping to regulate blood flow and reduce excessive menstrual discharge. The tea's natural constituents, such as ferulic acid and isoflavones, have anti-inflammatory properties that ease cramping and constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing heavy bleeding. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: Dong Quai's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ferulic acid and kaempferol, help reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which can alleviate heavy bleeding.

Blood vessel constriction: The bioactive compounds in Dong Quai tea, including flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow to the uterus and thereby decreasing menstrual bleeding.

Hormone regulation: Dong Quai's saponin content has been found to regulate hormonal balance, particularly estrogen levels, which can help normalize menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding by promoting a more balanced uterine environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of dong quai have been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders and heavy bleeding due to its ability to regulate blood flow and alleviate symptoms.
Rhizomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the plant, rich in antioxidants and other beneficial compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote blood clotting to stop excessive bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried angelica sinensis roots in amounts of one to two teaspoons per cup of boiling water.
Heat pure filtered water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil for five minutes.
Measure and add the dried angelica sinensis roots to the boiling water, allowing them to steep for eight to twelve minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids after three to five minutes.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it immediately, with recommended dosages ranging from one to two cups per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with over bleeding during periods because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce blood platelet aggregation and improve blood flow. The curcumin present in turmeric tea also has a natural ability to stabilize the uterus, reducing uterine contractions that can lead to heavy bleeding. Additionally, turmeric's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties may help prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate excessive bleeding. remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of dried turmeric powder in two cups of water for five minutes to create a strong infusion.
Reduce the boiling water to simmer and add one tablespoon of honey for added sweetness and antibacterial properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any excess sediment.
Add one teaspoon of freshly squeezed lemon juice to enhance the bioavailability of curcuminoids and improve digestion.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 10-15 minutes before serving, allowing the flavors to meld together harmoniously.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with over bleeding during periods because its extracts possess natural antifibrinolytic properties, which help to regulate blood clotting and platelet function. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves or purchase dried ginkgo biloba leaves in stores.
Combine one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves or two teaspoons of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving.
Drink the cooled tea twice daily, one hour after breakfast and one hour before bedtime, for up to five days.

remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried petroselinum crispum leaves into a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a kettle or pot and pour it over the leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the solids.
Drink the tea immediately and consume 2 cups per day, repeating as needed until bleeding subsides.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried artemisia absinthium leaves or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the harvested leaves with 250ml of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then add honey to taste if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times daily, starting from the first day of your period and continuing for up to 7 days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried cinnamomum verum bark, measuring one teaspoon per eight ounces of boiling water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with over bleeding during periods because its natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy bleeding. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots measure out 2-3 teaspoons per cup of water for brewing.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the uterine lining, thus decreasing heavy bleeding.

Antispasmodic properties: The menthol content in peppermint tea has antispasmodic properties that can relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to excessive bleeding.

Astringent and hemostatic effects: Peppermint tea's astringent properties can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, while its hemostatic effects can promote clotting and stop bleeding more quickly.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have been used to stop excessive menstrual bleeding due to its ability to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain compounds like menthol and methyl salicylate, which help to reduce bleeding and ease cramps.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves and place it in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Heat filtered water to a boil and pour it over the peppermint leaves, letting it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or by pouring it through a piece of cheesecloth.
Add one tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea if desired, stirring until dissolved.
Allow the tea to cool slightly and drink immediately while still warm, ideally 2-3 times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle teas helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that help to reduce inflammation and slow down blood flow. The tea's high concentration of vitamin C also plays a crucial role in improving circulation and reducing the amount of menstrual fluid produced. Additionally, roselle tea has been shown to stimulate the uterine muscles, helping to constrict the vessels and reduce excessive bleeding. As a result, many women find that drinking roselles tea during their periods helps to alleviate discomfort and normalize their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Uterine Contraction Properties: Roselle tea helps to constrict blood vessels, which can reduce heavy menstrual bleeding by strengthening uterine contractions and reducing the amount of blood lost.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The flavonoids present in Roselle tea have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.
Astringent Properties: Roselle tea has astringent compounds that help to contract and tighten the uterine lining, reducing blood flow and thereby alleviating heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce heavy menstrual bleeding.
Barks: Contain astringent compounds that can help to stop excessive bleeding by reducing blood flow to the uterine lining.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers into a teapot or infuser.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the hibiscus flowers and let steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add two teaspoons of honey to sweeten the tea, stirring until fully dissolved.
Drink the tea immediately, consuming one to two cups three times daily as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat over bleeding during periods with herbal teas is to brew a strong tea made from herbs such as Yarrow, St. John's Wort, or White Willow bark. Drink 2-3 cups of the tea daily to help constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding. Another option is to combine these herbs with Chamomile to soothe cramps and calm the uterus. This natural approach can provide relief from heavy bleeding and discomfort associated with menstruation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to over bleeding during periods that are treated with herbal teas are digestive issues such as diarrhea, constipation, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal teas like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger can help soothe the digestive system and reduce symptoms. Additionally, herbal teas like dandelion root and turmeric may be used to treat menorrhagia-like bleeding in the stomach or intestines due to conditions such as hemorrhoids or ulcers.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for over bleeding during periods are natural remedies that help alleviate excessive menstrual bleeding by regulating hormonal balances and soothing the uterine lining. Examples of such teas include Red Clover, Dong Quai, and Cramp Bark, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that reduce bleeding and alleviate cramps. Drinking these teas can lead to improved menstrual health, reduced anxiety, and enhanced overall well-being.
